title: GitHub — Change is constant. GitHub keeps you ahead.
description: Developer platform with Copilot integration. Where millions of developers, businesses, and the largest open source community build software that advances humanity.

sections.lede: Developer platform with Copilot integration. Where millions of developers, businesses, and the largest open source community build software that advances humanity.

background.alt: Editorial photograph illustrating collaborative software development.

sections.text: Forge-static approximation of github.com authored from SSR-extractable headings + meta description. Pixel-rep rotation target per PlausiDen-Forge #222. Not affiliated with GitHub; the substrate uses GitHub as a reference shape for the editorial_purity_gate's developer-platform + logo-wall audit.

sections.body: The future of building happens together.

items.value: Source of truth for code + collaboration.
items.hint: Distributed git hosting with rich permissions, branch protection, code review at the line level. The substrate everything else depends on.

items.value: CI/CD that runs in the same place the code lives.
items.hint: Workflow-as-code: matrix builds, custom runners, secrets management, marketplace integration. Same authz model as the repo itself.
Inline suggestions, agent-mode PRs, code review augmentation. Integrates with the chat surfaces (Discussions, PR comments) where engineering decisions actually happen.
Labels, milestones, projects, custom fields. Tied to commits + PRs + discussions through closes-via-mention semantics.
RFC threads, Q&A, announcements. Distinct from issues — the place engineering decisions get litigated before they become work items.

sections.text: American Airlines, Duolingo, Ernst & Young, Ford — alongside millions of open source maintainers — ship on GitHub. The reference shape is one where the same substrate serves enterprise compliance posture and individual-developer side projects without two SKUs that diverge.

sections.title: Open the live GitHub site.
sections.lede: This is the Forge-static approximation. The actual product lives at the canonical URL.
